text.title: rate-limiting Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rate-limiting as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: In-memory (dict-based) rate limiter deployed with more than one replica.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each replica has independent state. A user can hit N replicas sequentially and send N times the configured limit before any single replica records a violation. Always use Redis/Valkey for distributed state.

failure-mode.description: Per-IP rate limiter behind a CDN using request.client.host for the key.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Behind a CDN, request.client.host is the CDN edge IP. All users share one counter; when any user hits the limit, all users are throttled. Parse X-Forwarded-For with a trusted-proxy list.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: IPv6 users can rotate IPs within their /64 subnet trivially. Per-address limits allow effectively unlimited requests by rotating through the /64 address space.

failure-mode.description: Double-limiting: edge (Cloudflare/Kong) AND application-layer limiters on the same route without coordination.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Edge and app limits may have different windows and counts, causing confusing 429s that don't match the documented limits. Designate one canonical layer per route and document it.

failure-mode.description: Token bucket without min(bucket_size, ...) clamp on refill.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the clamp, long idle periods accumulate unlimited tokens. A user idle for 1 hour against a 100/s bucket can burst 360,000 requests, defeating the bucket size limit entirely.

failure-mode.description: Agent silently raises a limit because a load test failed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Limits encode security and cost decisions, not just performance ones. Raising a limit because a load test hit it may open an abuse vector. Human review is required for any limit change on auth and billing endpoints.

failure-mode.description: Missing integration test for the 429 path.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The happy path is always tested; the 429 path is usually not. Broken Retry-After parsing in clients is a common source of retry storms. Always test the 429 response and its headers in integration tests.
